Seed Library/Herb/Narrow-leaf Echinacea
HerbGreat Plains — native
nativemedicinalprairieperennial
Asteraceae

Echinacea angustifolia

Narrow-leaf Echinacea

The original medicinal coneflower of the American prairie, with narrow petals of soft rose-pink and an intensely resinous, tongue-numbing root.

$9.00per packet (~80 seeds)

Ships within 3 business days · Free shipping over $40

Days to Maturity

Blooms year 3

Difficulty

Patient

Harvest Window

July – August

Documented Since

Native

Provenance

The more potent of the two medicinal echinaceas, angustifolia was used by Plains nations long before it appeared in any European pharmacopoeia. Slower-growing and more demanding than its popular cousin purpurea, it rewards the patient gardener with a permanent, drought-resistant presence and a root that intensifies with every passing year.

Growing Notes

Requires cold stratification — sow in fall or refrigerate moist seeds for 30 days before spring sowing. Slow to establish (often blooms in year two or three) but extremely long-lived. Once settled, virtually indestructible.

The Marigold Society

A Vermont seed library, pressed-flower atelier, and quarterly journal for those who believe the old ways are worth preserving.
